insights.summary: Based on your EyeGuardian app data, it appears that you're experiencing eye strain and poor posture. Your blink rate of 13.2 blinks/min is below the healthy range (15-20 blinks/min), indicating reduced tear production and increased dry eye risk. Additionally, your screen distance of 42.0 cm is shorter than the recommended 50-70 cm, which may contribute to eye strain. It's essential to address these issues to prevent long-term eye health consequences.
insights.improvements: to reduce eye strain and improve posture
tips: Adjust your screen distance to the recommended 50-70 cm to reduce eye strain.

Blink regularly every 20-30 minutes to maintain tear production and prevent dry eyes. You can also try the 20-20-20 rule: every 20 minutes, look away from your screen and focus on something 20 feet away for 20 seconds.
Monitor your ambient brightness and adjust it to the healthy range (100-180). Lower or higher brightness levels can cause eye strain and fatigue.
Be mindful of your posture throughout the day, aiming for a score above 85% (currently 72.5%). Regular breaks and reminders can help you maintain good posture.
